title: Cord Tangles and Has Right Angle Plug
text:
Cons:
1. The cord is not silicone wire. It tangles easily.
2. Right angle mini-plug puts excess stress on the wire. I expect it to fail. With straight mini plugs, I can install a piece of shrink tubing over the plug to provide increased stress relief where the wire goes into the plug. Can't fit shrink tubing onto a right angle plug.
3. Size is a bit too large for comfortable sleeping. I use earbuds specifically to help me get to sleep by putting a droning podcast on at very low volume. Out like a light in a few minutes. The earbuds also act like earmuffs to mute room noise. These Panasonic buds are just a bit too long and are uncomfortable in the ear that's against the pillow.
4. Audio quality is so-so but adequate for voice.
5. Expensive for what they are. Can find four sets for less $$ elsewhere.

Pros:
1. The buds are angled such that you can see in daylight and feel in the dark which one is left and which is right.

After they break

title: Buy these... trust me... you're welcome.
text:
There were 32,000+ verified purchases on this set of earbuds, and at the low, low price of $7.96, I figured if they weren't any good, well, it wasn't the end of the world.

These are replacing a set of Bose earbuds that I've had for a couple of years, but they just wore out. I'm not going to pretend that Panasonic and Bose are in the same realm of sound quality. I will make the argument these are a better set of earbuds if you don't need complete reproduction of the sound.

First of all, these are way more comfortable than my Bose. They fit in my ear better. The Bose had the advantage of three-point contact inside my ear, whereas the Panasonic simply goes into the ear canal. But, the Panasonic is softer and gentler inside my ear.

Second of all, the Panasonic does a superior job of blocking outside noise. The Bose was advertised as "noise reducing" and they weren't very good on that point. However, I always dismissed that because of the quality of

title: One Star
text: These are not worth anything. They did not last but one day with my 7 year old daughter.

title: Wanted to like these but....
text: My husband is a music guy and a sound guy. He really knows and appreciates good quality of both those things. When he needed a pair of small in ear headphones I did LOTS of research with Amazon reviews to find a pair that reviewed well with our very limited budget. Even though these headphones were even less expensive then I was willing to pay they got great reviews so I ordered the first pair to go with his birthday present. He was very impressed with the quality vs. price AT FIRST. Within one week they were not sounding as good and by the end of week two they were completely broke. We decided maybe we got a bad pair so I recently purchased another pair. This pair didn't last one week before the left ear bud completely stopped working. We are returning these and trying a different pair. For the price we would have been thrilled with mediocre quality but they didn't work at all.

title: You have to break these in. Takes a while but once you do, WOW!!!
text:
Have some patience with these. I needed a new pair of plugs since my five year old Altec Lansings started falling apart. I tried many others as replacements, but none were as good as the Lansings, most notably on the bass side. I read the reviews about these and figured what the heck.
When I first got them and fitted them to my ears, they were terrible. Why would Panasonic make this junk? 2 stars at best. Bass was horrible. But as time went by, I started to notice that not only the bass but the high end frequencies were getting better. Now I am more than satisfied!!! These even beat my old Lansings.
In short, have some patience. These need a break in period. You won't be sorry.

title: Way Better Than Expected
text: I like having a pair of wired headphones in the house, nice to not worry about having to charge your headphones. I was purposely looking for headphones that are more flat on the outer part, so it makes it a little more comfortable to sleep with, and also use with multiple devices. Not expecting much in the way of sound, often the wired headphones have more treble and are usually good for audiobooks, or voice, more than music. I hooked these up with an adapter for a couple of different tablets, phone, and laptop. They sounded different on different devices, makes sense, so my phone, wow awesome bass, in a wired headphone? Okay, cool, and on my tablets with turning off my equalizer, the sound was crisp and clear and still had some bass, but great too for podcasts. I like how snug these fit in the ear, but I changed out the little plastic ear covers for some others I have, and I have to say, that snug fit makes them much better with more noise being filtered out. The longer cord is good t

title: The best bang for you buck - bar none
text:
Let me start off by saying that I have used many, many different earbuds and headphones over the years, and these are by FAR the best bang for my buck in terms of quality, durability, and comfort. I've always managed to land jobs where I'm able to listen to audio content throughout my workday, and have consequently spent most of my working hours with at least one earbud tucked into my ears.

I thread these down to my pocket where they are plugged into my iPod full time. No matter how much running, walking, lifting, jumping or moving around I do (and I work as a package delivery guy, which means hundreds of movements and ten miles of walking a day), these things take ages to wear out. Under normal use, I'm sure they'd never need replacing unless you managed to blow out the speaker somehow, or cause blatant damage to them. They don't cut in and out from wear on the jack end or on the headphone ends like some other earbuds, which will give way after being cranked around a bit.

title: Clear sound with great trebile and bass
text: They sound great. They look great. The wire and the input jack seem to feel a little bit average, cheap, hard to the touch but it doesn't hurt the durability of them and I do not need a gold spoon just great sound.

title: My Lamentable Love Stories with Earbuds
text:
Spouse often says I'm rough on earphones. I'm certain that I've never beat up any of them, nor have I scolded them for anything they did or did not do. In fact, when it comes to earphones, I've always acted like a dotting parent or sometimes, smitten admirer. I've always started off loving them to bits and they loved me right back for a while, then they grew up (headphone years are like dog years, you blink once and it's terrible twos, blink twice and it's rebellious teens, so the honeymoon usually did not last very long) and became obnoxious things. I have tolerated a lot of bad behaviours, but when they let the precious connection between the ear buds and the cord fraying away (and they always do), I have to put my foot down. Time to open the wallet once more.

I can't count how many pairs of earphones have passed through my life that way. All with pedigree or well-known parentage -- Apple, Sony, Panasonic, Skullcandy, Dre, Plantronics, Koss, Philips, JVC, Pioneer, etc -- a

title: Good for the price - don't expect a miracle.
text:
They are plenty good for the price. Sound quality is good enough, and you can definitely spend more and get worse.

That said, don't buy these after reading the wirecutter reviews, and expect to get audiophile quality. They sound good...for the price.

The remote has a flat spot where the button is, which makes it easy to find without looking. A pair of Sony's I bought didn't, which was a pretty big minus after using these.

title: Great price, cute earbuds
text: At this great price, I always keep a couple of extra sets in my purse for when my kids lose or break their earbuds. The sound is quite good.

title: totally worthless
text: there was no battery. I had trouble putting on a smaller ear piece so they would fit in my ears. I finally did that but it did not cancel out any noise and I could not hear anything out of one earpiece.  I am stuck with them because the company won't take them back.  DO NOT BUY!!!!

title: "One Great Pair of Ear Buds Worth EVERY Penny!"
text: I Love these ear buds! It's great value for the money and the sound  is really good. Although it's not big on the bass but the perfect balance of the treble level makes up for that. It has the right balance of bass and treble and it's really good if you use it as your primary ear bud as a replacement for your Roku ear buds. You could actually use these also if you have a CD or MP 3 Player.....its slick and well hidden inside of your ears and you can actually sleep with these if your head is lying down sideways on your pillow and unlike some other ear buds when you get that annoying feeling after a while....you won't feel this with this ear buds. Real good quality made ear buds and highly recommended, you should get one and all the reviewers were right about this: It's one really good pair for the money and worth your while.

title: HOLY COW! Exceeded all expectations in any price range.
text:
I so wasn't expecting this caliber of sound from such inexpensive earbuds. I use these at work when I need to view videos without forcing my entire office to listen along and needed to find an alternative to my Apple ear pods because they always fall out of my ears.

I tested them out by listening higher fidelity version of my favorite Steely Dan songs and OMG I wasn't expecting this. These super cheap Panasonic ErgoFit In-Ear Earbud Headphones are fantastic and deliver big sound. I heard nuances in the music that I've never noticed before. The sound is balanced, and I even had to turn the volume down on my computer because they are very efficient (loud).

I am not an audiophile, but it sounded perfectly balanced to my ears. The bass isn't boomy, and the mid-range and highs are crystal clear. They sound better than our larger Sonos sound system. In fact, I needed to drown it out because my musical tastes differ widely from my crew.

The cord was plenty lon

title: Unreliable
text: I will be returning these. They are less than a week old with me and they turn off while in use on a phone call on full charge. Very unreliable.

title: Yep. They're Earbuds.
text: My son goes through earbuds like you wouldn't believe. He has ASD. He needs them to function in the world. And he fidgets them constantly. I've tried every cheap set out there (after learning hard lessons with expensive ones - Bose earbuds are worth the dough if you're not going to rip them to pieces by picking at them and twirling them and whatnot). Panasonics sound good for the money and last - usually - up to a few months. Recommend.

title: Incredible for the price
text: I use these mostly to listen to podcasts and music on the subway.  They are comfortable, block out a lot of sound (important on the subway), and sound great.  Good price too.

title: Sure glad I switched to these.
text: Has not failed. Love these. I returned an expensive, new-on-the-market brand promising all sorts of action and features, for my Panasonic cheaper earphones. I'm sure glad I did. Expensive does NOT always mean better.

title: Color me DELIGHTED!!!!!!
text: I was not expecting much from these little tiny earbuds aside from a way to listen to my music when I need to be more aware of my surroundings and just want to block out the idiots on the subway, but when I actually checked them out I was SHOCKED by how good and LOUD these lil babies are! Great bass, great depth and detail in the sound for such a low, low price. These will remain in my purse when I don't want to wear the big cans. I LOVE them.

title: Still working
text: My son uses these at school. I’m surprised he hasn’t destroyed them yet. They have REALLY held up.

title: The Best Budget Earphones w/Mic
text:
I purchased these earbuds as just a pair to keep in the car and they turned into my daily drivers. I had a pair of nice quality, good sounding Samsung earbuds that I received as a gift. After one too many times of either forgetting the Samsung ones at home or leaving them in the office I purchased these Panasanic Ergofit earbuds as a backup.
When I received these in the mail, I popped them in my ear only to be disappointed by the fit. That is until I realized a little trick of putting them in my ear slightly turned to the back and gently wiggling them until they almost "lock" in place. Once I did this I quickly found that these are some of the most comfortable earbuds I have ever used. I generally can't wear earbuds for long due to earache/headache problems, but I can wear these all day long. I have even used these while running and they have stayed in place pretty well.
The audio quality is right up there as well with great volume and range with very clear sound. The

title: Hard to beat for the price.
text:
All I can say is "Amazing"! I have been using the non-microphone version of these earbuds for several years now [[ASIN:B003EM8008 Panasonic ErgoFit In-Ear Earbud Headphones RP-HJE120-K (Black) Dynamic Crystal Clear Sound, Ergonomic Comfort-Fit]] and when I saw that they now had a version with a microphone I couldn't wait to try them out. They did not disappoint.

Before I continue, remember that these are under $20 and the non-microphone version are under $15. With that price, your first impression will usually be "cheap and crappy". While the parts are of the cheaper variety and the cable could stand to be a bit heavier, the sound quality is amazing - for cheap earbuds. I have many varieties and classes of in-ear monitors with these being the cheapest. These cheap in-ear monitors are within about 95% of the sonic quality (to my ears) of my more expensive Etymotec in-ear monitors. The have great bass response and smooth mids and highs due to the large drivers.

title: Unbelievable sound quality and completely reasonable microphone. Tremendous value.
text:
These earbuds are startlingly good. They punch *way* above their weight. I've used these almost daily for a couple months and have been very pleased.

The bass is rich, the separation reasonable and the treble bright without being shrill. With some music, the bass was a little heavy, but that was easily resolved with a minor EQ adjustment. The sound is way better than you would expect at this low price.

Phone calls are pretty good. If you hold the mic up a little bit, the sound quality is great. If you let the mic dangle, it sounds similar to a conference room speaker phone - decent and intelligible, but not great. Of course, "conference room speaker phone" quality is totally acceptable in professional environments, so these earbuds will actually meet the expectations of the people you're talking to.

The fit is good. I found the combination of rubber ends that fit me well and they are comfortable for all day use.

Overall, these are a

title: Great Product
text: I always break my earphones. Whether it's from the vacuum cleaner, dropping them (and the iPod, which survived the fall intact), or the wires becoming disconnected at the base, earphones never seem to last long for me. I have not had these for very long, but I have a reason to believe things will be different this time. Today I accidentally ran them through the washing machine and dryer, and they still work perfectly! Additionally, they feel nice in the ear, seem to have good sound quality for the price (although I'm no expert on these matters), and come in lots of cute colors. If you need a set of earphones, get these!

title: Not a great set of headphones
text:
I knew these were on the cheap end but they came up a couple times in lists of good headphones for small ears. One good feature is the L shaped headphone jack with a small housing so it fits into narrow phone case openings. They have multiple sizes of earbud covers to fit different size ear canals.
The bad things: They have no noise cancelation at all. The sound is just ok. My biggest issue is that the wires are extremely flimsy. There actually may be a short in it and I only just opened the package. When the plug turns in the jack, there is a ton of crackling.

If you are looking for basic, budget headphones with a mic and not high quality, they will be fine, but probably won't last long.

title: OK earphones at this low price
text:
These earbuds are nothing special. Their best attribute is that they are cheap. The sound from them is nothing special. In comparison to my standard white IPod buds these seem to emphasize the higher frequencies. There seems to be nothing happening in the bass range with these. I don't think they are efficient enough to have much bass at all. To be fair, my IPod buds overly emphasize bass so they are not that accurate either. I also compared these to a pricy Sennheiser set that I've owned for a couple of years and let's just say there is a reason why the Sennheisers cost more.

Surely these Panasonic buds are very nice to use if you think they might get damaged somehow in use as it will not break your heart to have to replace them. These might be especially useful if you only listen to talk radio or have high frequency hearing loss. If you really like to listen to the most of your music and you must wear earbuds then these are not for you.

I also have to note that

title: So Comfortable She Forgets They Are In Her Ear
text: After using these for a month my wife has finally decided to let me know how they are.  She says they feel comfortable in her ear to the point where she often has forgotten she has them in.  She uses them to watch [[ASIN:B0089BPEZS HBO GO]] on her Kindle Fire.

title: Surprisingly waterproof
text: I discovered completely by accident that these will survive going through the laundry.  So one I have one in each pair of shorts that I wear.  They just live in the cargo pocket so I always have some on me.  My only complaint is the packaging is hard to open.  It takes me a few minutes and usually makes me mad.  I'd love it if these came in a frustration free variety.

title: After 1 month stopped working
text:
I have had these ear buds for 1 month and the right ear bud stopped working. Amazon says we cannot return
Frustrating

title: My backup wired ear buds on the cheap
text: Really like these wired ear buds for when the more expensive wireless ones are not appropriate. I tend to use these in environments where there is a good possibility of damaging the other ones. the sound is decent for such an inexpensive product. I bought a spare right away once I saw how good there were. The first ones lasted over three years before the glue that kept the actual speaker/ear piece connected to base of the ear bud gave way. Glad to have that spare then and have since purchased another spare so I won't ever have to go without. Good 'insurance policy' to help keep those expensive ones from being lost (try mowing the lawn) or damaged (try being caught in the rain on a walk).

title: Decent ear phones
text: Decent audio at a great price.

title: Purple
text: Great quality, budget friendly, pretty purple color. There's an even cheaper version without the microphone that's equally good if you don't need the mic.

title: Five Stars
text: For the price of these I couldn't believe the quality sound that they produced.

title: I love these, they hold up to a lot of ...
text: I love these, they hold up to a lot of wear, including me washing them by accident in the washing machine. They have 2 sets of the the poertion that goes in the ear,so you can get a good fit. They are comfortable and a great price

title: Just what I expected
text: These earbuds are okay, I did not expect anything less but they definitely helped while my bigger ones broke. Still hoping to repair those but int he meantime these are good and they came with replacements for the earbuds and well packaged.

title: Cheap and Work Well
text: Cheap and work well.  I bought these because they were cheap and I'm always tearing mine up during weed eating season.  I figured I would buy a few pairs of these and see if they would hold me over.  They seem to work well and I have not had any issues.  They may lack bass but they hold up rather well, especially for the price.

title: Decent headphones
text: I got these to listen to music through my laptop. I'm no expert but they're decent headphones, good sound, nothing special. The thin wire makes them feel cheap and the kinks in the wire from being wrapped up inside the packaging feel like they'll never go away. The wire also gets a lot of knots and tangles.

title: Tragus piercing appoved
text: Great value for the money.  The buds go right up behind your tragus really blocking any extra sound.  For those of us with a tragus piercing, it does work fine, but my earring starts to ache after maybe 45 minutes.  But it does not slip out even with the tragus stud.  The wire seemed like poorer quality than I was anticipating--thicker and flat rather than thin and roundish like the more current models--but that is more of a style critique than one of functionality.  The headphones have worked well for the few weeks that I have been using it.  If you're looking for something that will stay in your ear and block out all other sound, producing high sound quality, this is a great model to get, especially for the price.

title: Sent wrong color!
text: Used to purchase these a lot and I needed another pair. Good for the price. However, these are CLEARLY not metallic. They are glossy. Disappointed!

title: One Star
text: It said iPhone compatible but the adapter doesn't connect ti iPhone.

title: Panasonic makes good products
text: These have lasted longer. I go through ear buds like crazy, but Panasonic makes good products. So, I got one pair for my Mac, and another for my little radio.

title: affordable with great sound. Assembly could be improved to last years
text: Wow, affordable with great sound. Assembly could be improved to last years.

title: Good clear sound
text: I love that these are comfortable for me. Others have left my ears sore. Plus, other ones that cost less break almost immediately. These are much more durable.

title: 5 stars
text: Great for the price. Decent quality

title: I use the smallest ones and they stay put in my ears easily. I can use them on a treadmill or ...
text: I have a hard time keeping buds in my ears because the buds always seem to be too large. These come with a few size options. I use the smallest ones and they stay put in my ears easily. I can use them on a treadmill or on a plane and they are comfortable. I have two pairs and they have both held up nicely over time. I prefer them to my husbands more expensive ones.

title: Great but...
text: Only reason I wouldn't give these 5 stars is the input end?(not sure what it's called) The input portion that you would put into the phone is "L" shaped versus "l". I have to buy a new phone case so that I can use the headphones with the case on. Other than that, these headphones are fantastic! Crystal clear sound with minimal volume, and I don't even mind that they are in-ear.

title: Two Stars
text: They are headphones that perform very poorly with regards to other headphones I have purchased from Amazon.

title: You won't go wrong with these
text: I love these earbuds. Incredible sound.

title: Good deal
text: Love these little earbuds. Work great, and cheap enough to get a few more for backups.

title: More junk ear buds STOPPED IN 4 months... Same as usual
text: Ok for a few months then as usual the left side STOPPED WORKING!!!! More worthless products (Earbuds)-

title: I definitely recommend to my family and friends
text:
Quick delivery, product matches description and ease of order.
I definitely recommend to my family and friends.

title: Five Stars
text: Item arrived earlier than promised and it is as advertised

title: Hurt my ear
text: These have ok sound but fit into my ear funny. I cant really use. Might just be the shape of my ear but they hurt. If they fit your ear, the sound is great.

title: Great sound for little ear buds
text: I love these. They match my iPod Pelican case and my Big Grips iPad case. The sound is great and it comes with different size tips so you can make them fit your ears. The sound is awesome. I would recommend these to anyone who wants a nice set of inexpensive ear buds.

title: Almost Five Stars For Panasonic Headphones
text: Sturdy and extraordinary quality. Would have had five stars if there was a volume adjustor.  Love love love the color.

title: not about that bass
text: Not for people who like a lot of bass, like ME. They last, they're inexpensive, and the cat loves playing with them after I've worn them out.

title: Nice earbuds
text: These earbuds were fine. They were lightweight. They were comfortable for my daughter.  The quality was fine. They stayed in okay. They are plain so they would work for anyone. I like that they are Microphone and Call Controller Compatible. I would recommend them.

title: So far so good.
text: So far, these have worked great. They fit and stay put. The sound is above average. We go thru these so fast, so this price point is decent too.

title: Well made and comfortable, the best cheap earbud on the market
text:
I normally work with earbuds in all day and my ears would start to hurt from the added discomfort. Not since I bought these! They come with three different silicone ear tips to fit to your exact ear canal size. The tiny size fits easily into your ear and stays there or hours without notice. Works seamlessly with my iPhone for making calls or listening to podcasts, the pause button to start and stop whatever you're listening to is really nice. The microphone operates clearly.

For the price you can't do better, these aren't cheaply made and hold up to daily use. Considering the abuse my earbuds go through these hold up remarkably well and when once pair wears out after a year or so I simply buy a new one. I made my girlfriend into a convert, she loves and swears by her pair now too!

title: Great ear fit
text: Ergonomic shape makes for fitting in ear better compared to other headphones. Good sound quality and durability when used for listening to music while working out.

title: Decent quality - NOT AUDIOPHILE - for much lower price than expected.
text: Wow these sound totally okay for the price. You're not going to be wowed by detail or stage size or anything. OTOH I am wowed by the balance and friendless of the sound. No listening fatigue whatsoever. There is nothing about using these that actively bothers me, which, combined with the extreme low price really earns the highest possible rating.

title: Died after 2 months.
text:
Love this earphone but one side stop working after using for 2 months.
I bought 3 of them and this is the only one that broke that fast. I guess this is just the unlucky one then.

title: pretty comfortable!
text: they dont fall off, pretty comfortable!! Surprisingly good quality sound. 100% recommend

title: Great for extra small ears
text: I've bought three pairs of these over the years.  Every time I lose them (or one of my kids steals them), I decide to try something new.  I have yet to find a pair that fits my ears like these do.

title: Five Stars
text: My favorite earbuds out there.  The smaller fit is just a nice option.

title: When Your Second Pair is as Important as the First
text: I try to take a mile to mile-and-a-half walk each morning with our dog, to keep both our weights down, and since I walk the same route every day, after a while it gets a bit monotonous. Sure I can watch the weeds at the side of the road as they progress through the seasons--presently the privet and the honeysuckle are in sweet bloom, later on I want to see if the thistle plant blooms this year, in the fall the leaves make a carpet of color--but most of the time, dull, dull, dull. So I've taken to listening to a podcast while I walk, and was using the nice set of Jabra earphones I have, but they are too heavy to walk with; they work fine if I'm sitting still but if I move they fall out of my ear (I do mean my ear, because the traffic is so bad and I have to cross a busy street, I only keep one in). So I wanted a light pair to walk the dog in. These are great! The sound is nice and clear and very full even if I only have one in, and I love the fact that three sizes of earpads are include

title: works great for what I need
text:
I really never have used earbuds much at all before. I do take my iPhone or iPad with me when I am going to be sitting and waiting for Dr's offices or mechanic visits. I like to watch videos but don't want to use the speaker function, so these earbuds work great for me to listen in private.

I am sorry that I don't have much experience with other earbuds. I can't compare this set to others but I like these and am happy with them. They work great for what I need.

I have no complaints with these and they are great for my own needs. Thanks.

title: Sound is great but mic didnt work at all.
text:
I was so happy to get these headphones and today I used it on my first conference but my mic didnt work correctly at all. I should have gotten the regular ones without the mic. But I cant return it now because I threw the box out.

$22 down the drain. I can't believe that. Just get the ones without the mic.

title: Great quality for the price
text:
If you're looking for a cheap pair of headphones that you don't have to be super upset if you lose, or a spare pare to keep somewhere just in case...these are it! For the price, they sound quite good and they seem to be very durable. Sure, you're not going to get the same audio quality as a $60 pair of earbuds, but these sound very good, not overly tinn-y and with a good bass. If I lose this pair, I will buy another just like it. I'm quite pleased with it.
Also love that it comes with multiple sizes of earbud attachments, as I have small ears.

title: No Bueno!
text: I have the Bose Sound Ultra for Android and they sound amazing to me.  BUT, the cord keeps fraying at the junction of the plug that goes into my phone.  I just had to order my 9th pair through Best Buy (who is no longer going to carry them for Android...only IOS).  wanted was an economical pair to have in my backpack for "emergency".  I ordered three different ones to see what sounded good to use until I find a permanent WIRED earphone with a tough cord made to survive the life of New York City with delicious sound.  These things ... THESE THINGS.... they will be sent back tomorrow.  They are HORRIBLE.  I wasn't expecting to be wowed for the price.  But the reviews made me think I might get somewhat of a "wow".  Seriously, I cannot even believe I allowed myself to have hope.  I can only hope Robert Smith of The Cure will forgive me for using his music to test these made for a six year old. There you have it.  I wouldn't accept these things to listen to a voice read a bo

title: Standard earbuds — wired
text: You’ll need to plug these into your phone with the audio jack. This is NOT a lightning connector  and it is NOT wireless but if you can still use earbuds that plug in these fit fine and the sound is decent. I don’t think they are stable enough for a lot of moving around but if you are more or less stable, these will do the trick. Earbuds have enough options and I found them to be very comfortable.

title: Like these so much I bought 3 pairs
text:
These are the only earbuds that actually fit in my ear comfortably and don't fall out when I move my head.
I still like the kind that have a behind the ear clip if I run but it isn't convenient if you are taking them on and off alot. These are perfect inexpensive earbuds for everyday use. Fun colors too!

title: Cheap and "OK"
text: These are kind of fragile, but work. Bass isn't good, not the greatest sound replication, but for the price there's no complaining. Except I also bought a pair off Amazon that cost a whopping $3 and they work the same. Same sound, same quality. Skullcandy is the one to beat, but I don't like using those where they might get wet or destroyed.

title: Quality of sound is stunning!
text: Works just like everyone else said. Since I am 6'2", it is a tad bit on the short side (the cable length) but besides that tiny issue, its great! Sounds are balanced but do not skimp on the bass.

title: worked great :)
text: Hubby bought me a galaxy x tablet and I love it but the volume is very low.... These took care of the problem... Thanks :) they came VERY quickly!! Great customer service!!

title: Tinny with no Bass
text:
These sound better than the buds that came with my mp3 player. The smallest earpad is too small for adults and will probably fit children. The other two are adult sized. The design of these is a little odd as the earpad is offset a little bit from the bud. This unconventional design may bother you if you have very sensative ears. Jan 2012.

Update Sep 2012.
Panasonic must have changed suppliers. I purchased a second pair, and while they looked the same they sound horrible. About the only good thing is that they don't fall out of my ear. I cannot recommend this product.

title: Good value
text: They fit in the ear great and don't fall out. My only complaint would be that the cord is a little bit short

title: Great Headphones
text: Great pair of headphones for the prices. Cheap enough to replace when broken. I have 3 of them and typically keep one in my backpack, on connected to my laptop and one just for generally use. Pretty good sounding music and feels comfortable going into your ear. just brought another pair as of today for a friend.

title: Earwax
text: Creates a wet inner suctioning and buildup of earwax

title: Great Value
text: These earbuds have great sound, they fit in my ear comfortably and don't come out unless I want them to.  Great value for the price I paid.  I have paid over twice as much for similar earbuds and these sound way better.

title: Good product
text: This ear phone is awesome. Worth the money!

title: Good value.
text: Good headphones for what I need them for, which is the gym. Reasonably priced too.

title: So so
text: I gave these a try, but the bass isn't that great. My cheap Coby's are much better & that is what I'll stick with.

title: Nice quality
text: I use these with my phone for music or movies.  I don't bother anyone that way.

title: Not as comfy as I thought
text: Just got em today, so can't speak of their durability.  DEFINITELY NOT the bass capacity, but they're lower end. Not as comfy as I thought, even though my ears are small.  But my ear canals are sensitive.  I've had these types of ear buds before, but the other ones fit much better--but then again:  These are low-end ones, so I shouldn't complain.  Perhaps I'll try changing the size of the rubber ear plugs on them, that may help.  I'll have to take off one star for less comfort.

title: Great for the price
text: My $100 Bose earbuds finally died, so I bought these as a cheap replacement. The sound is pretty good. I certainly couldn't expect the bass and sound quality from the old ones, but these are definitely decent.

title: They work great
text: I got these are my on the gone headphones. They work great

title: These are fantastic. Im kind of an earphone snob
text: These are fantastic. Im kind of an earphone snob, but I dont have a lot of money. These worked better then most the super expensive ones I tried. Loud, good bass, and great at cancelling out noise. Highly recommend

title: New favorite headphones!
text: New favorite headphones!  Absolutely love them.. fit in ears great. Best headphones I've bought in the last year. No more searching!

title: Outstanding
text: Sound is loud and clear, price was great. What more could you ask for?

title: Good headphones for my tween son
text: Good headphones for my tween son. He likes them. Says they fit well and are comfortable to wear. Now if he can just keep up with them!

title: Love Them!!!
text: They last me for about 9 to 10 months until I have to get new ones. For $6 to $7 that really good.

title: Good buy, but my ears deserve better!
text:
Pros:
Nice length
Fit:
Stays in ear while exercising
Design:
Very lightweight. Comes in many color choices.

Cons:
Sound is is "just" ok for $10
Hurts inside of my ears after 15-20 minutes
Cord is very thin

Overall, its just good for emergency back ups but doesn't have the quality of the better headphones out there.

title: Too Big for 12 year Old's Ears.
text: Good headphones but still too large for my 12 year old's ears.  I wish they'd make them more kid sized.

title: RETURNING/DEFECTIVE/MISSING PARTS.
text: There were no adjustable ear pieces in with the packaging. The sound was broken/intermittent. Returning these ASAP!

title: Last long
text: These earbuds last for pretty long. I am pretty impressed. I think the price is reasonable for the quality

title: Pretty sweet sound and price
text: Bought these for my wife to use with her iPad and she likes them.  Got them on a sale price of around $7 with free shipping, and its hard to beat that deal.

title: Good Value
text: Use these at work which cancel the noise yet can still hear the business around you.  Good cord length from my computer but would prefer a wrap over ear style to ensure they don't come loose.  Comes with extra ear pieces for extended use.  Great product

title: Five Stars
text: Great for children and not a bad price.

title: Five Stars
text: Durable, affordable. Can't go wrong with these if you are on a budget.
